rejected_candidates.title: LamboToken implementation can be initialized and minted by anyone
rejected_candidates.reason: The code supports direct initialization of the implementation contract, but clone storage is separate and factory-created clones still initialize normally. The impact is limited to a counterfeit-looking standalone implementation token/social confusion, not protocol-level fund loss or invariant break.

rejected_candidates.title: Router has no ERC20 recovery path for stuck token balances
rejected_candidates.reason: Direct ERC20 transfers to almost any contract can become stuck, and no standard router flow was shown to leave recoverable ERC20 residuals. The native ETH recovery/dust issue is already tracked in F-015; this broader ERC20 version is not a distinct protocol-level vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Fee configuration permits 100% sell-fee confiscation and buy-side DoS
rejected_candidates.reason: This is an owner-controlled fee-policy parameter rather than a permissionless exploit. Users can observe feeRate and protect sells with minReturn; buy disablement or confiscatory fees require privileged admin action, so it is not retained as a reportable vulnerability.
